title: Warhammer Quest (1995) - Still the Gold Standard - Part 3 - Magazines and DIY Stuff
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4WHM_GRby90

Daniel (@dungeondive) closes the Warhammer Quest Master Class series with the publications and fan-created content that keep the game alive. White Dwarf issues 184-204 introduced special quest tiles (jail, Flames of Kazla), new events, dark secret cards, outdoor adventuring rules, harbour travel, sewer tiles, and the condensed Domain of the Horned Rat skaven expansion. Three issues of Death Blow magazine added banking systems, warriors, and adventures. Issue 208's retrospective reveals the planned chaos dwarf and full skaven expansions that never materialised.

The fan community—led by Little Monk—has surpassed Games Workshop's own output. His Ultimate Adventure Book compiles hundreds of quests combining all official content with original material. Additional releases include expanded travel hazards (108 entries), settlement events, an Ultimate Treasure Book, and full expansion-scale supplements: Hall of the Hag Queen (dark elf themed), Mountains of Mayhem (chaos dwarfs, completing the unrealised GW expansion), Realms of the Savage, Castle of Darkness, Caverns of Slaughter, and Carnival of Carnage. Daniel walks through his DIY tile-making process (print, spray-glue onto mounting board) and recommends the Warhammer Quest Customized Forum for access to Little Monk's files.

----

Has any other game's fan community matched what Little Monk and the WQ community have built?
